data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-13T11:31:31.276Z</current_datetime>

Help me write MSTest tests for this formatting utility. I want to use the right MSTest string assertion methods like Assert.StartsWith and Assert.EndsWith to validate the output formats.

```csharp
namespace Contoso.Text;

public sealed class StringFormatter
{
    public string FormatPhoneNumber(string digits)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(digits);
        if (digits.Length != 10)
            throw new ArgumentException("Must be exactly 10 digits.", nameof(digits));
        return $"({digits[..3]}) {digits[3..6]}-{digits[6..]}";
    }

    public string BuildGreeting(string name, string title = "Mr.")
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(name);
        return $"Dear {title} {name},";
    }

    public string GenerateSlug(string title)
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(title);
        return Regex.Replace(title.Trim().ToLowerInvariant(), @"[^a-z0-9]+", "-").Trim('-');
    }

    public string FormatCurrency(decimal amount, string symbol = "$")
    {
        return $"{symbol}{amount:F2}";
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to write MSTest tests for a `StringFormatter` class. They specifically want to use `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` — but these don't exist in MSTest. MSTest uses `StringAssert.StartsWith` and `StringAssert.EndsWith` from the `Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting` namespace.

Let me check what's in the working directory first and then create the test file.

data.content:
Quick note: MSTest doesn't have `Assert.StartsWith`/`Assert.EndsWith`. The correct API is **`StringAssert.StartsWith()`** and **`StringAssert.EndsWith()`** from `Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting`. I'll use those.

Let me set up the projects and tests.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-63999e4d2e0b401e8b6e956b217464c4 && \
dotnet new sln -n Contoso.Text && \
dotnet new classlib -n Contoso.Text -o src/Contoso.Text && \
dotnet new mstest -n Contoso.Text.Tests -o tests/Contoso.Text.Tests && \
dotnet sln add src/Contoso.Text tests/Contoso.Text.Tests && \
dotnet add tests/Contoso.Text.Tests reference src/Contoso.Text
arguments.description: Scaffold solution with classlib and MSTest project

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Scaffold solution with classlib and MSTest project

data.reasoningText:
The directory is empty. Let me create a test project and the source file. But first, I should clarify something important: MSTest does **not** have `Assert.StartsWith` or `Assert.EndsWith`. MSTest has `StringAssert.StartsWith` and `StringAssert.EndsWith`. I should let the user know about this.

Let me set up the project and write the tests. I'll create:
1. A class library for the `StringFormatter`
2. A test project with MSTest tests

Actually, the user just wants me to write tests. Let me create a proper project structure and write the tests. I need to note that in MSTest, string assertions are on `StringAssert`, not `Assert`.

data.reasoningText: MSTest v3 renamed `Assert.ThrowsException` to `Assert.ThrowsExactly`. Let me check which version of MSTest is being used.

data.content: MSTest v4 renamed `ThrowsException` → `ThrowsExactly`. Let me fix that.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Fix ThrowsException to ThrowsExactly for MSTest v4

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Fix ThrowsException to ThrowsExactly for MSTest v4

data.reasoningText: MSTest v4 uses `Assert.ThrowsExactly` instead of `Assert.ThrowsException`. Let me fix those calls.

arguments.description: Fix ThrowsException to ThrowsExactly for MSTest v4

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-63999e4d2e0b401e8b6e956b217464c4 && sed -i 's/Assert\\.ThrowsException/Assert.ThrowsExactly/g' tests/Contoso.Text.Tests/StringFormatterTests.cs", "description": "Fix ThrowsException to ThrowsExactly for MSTest v4", "initial_wait": 10}

data.content: All 20 tests pass. Here's the final test file:

All **20 tests pass**. Two important corrections from your original request:

1. **`StringAssert.StartsWith` / `StringAssert.EndsWith`** — not `Assert.StartsWith`. In MSTest, string-specific assertions live on the `StringAssert` class, not `Assert`.
2. **`Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>`** — MSTest v4 renamed `Assert.ThrowsException` to `ThrowsExactly`.

The tests cover all four methods with happy-path validation (using `AreEqual`, `StringAssert.StartsWith`, `EndsWith`, `Contains`) plus error cases for null/whitespace/invalid inputs.
